title: PVP on Hyperliquid
summary_title: Coordinated attack on Hyperliquid, validator response, and PVP trading's future
summary_description: Jelly crash, delisting decision, and why PVP.trade is building the social layer for Hyperliquid perps.

key_moments.label: Space begins: hosts discuss timing and recent Hyperliquid events
Zerk introduces rekt_gang; discussion of coordinated attack on Jelly
Noms (PVP.trade co-founder) describes Jelly position down 8-9 million, calls it coordinated attack
Discussion of OI caps relative to market cap and HLP risk exposure
PBR comments on Binance threat and validator response to delisting
Clarification that validators voted as committee, not just Jeff
Pivot to PVP.trade: 30 billion volume, Telegram bot, copy trading launch
Discussion of Hyperliquid EVM opportunities: lending, liquid staking, NFTs

pull_quote: Trading with friends is always more fun, and it feels less bad when you get liquidated because you're not just a retard trading by yourself.
